Excellent — below is a **complete gravity-first design translated into a practical Bill of Materials (BOM)** for a **53-foot reefer container** used as a **Yellow Perch breeding \+ hatchery \+ nursery core**, with **visual / thermal tanks elsewhere**.

This is written so you can:

* price it  
* hand it to a fabricator  
* phase it (buildable in stages)

I’ll flag **CRITICAL** items vs **OPTIONAL / FUTURE**.

---

## **SYSTEM SUMMARY (what this BOM supports)**

* **Container:** 53' insulated reefer  
* **Flow:** 100% gravity through fish → solids → bio → sump  
* **Pumps:** only lift clean water (low head, isolated)  
* **Use case:** broodstock conditioning, spawning, egg incubation, larvae, early nursery  
* **Visual tanks:** *not inside this container* (heat-only exchange later)

---

## **HIGH-LEVEL FLOW (gravity path)**

**Image**

**![Image][image1]**

**![Image][image2]**

**![Image][image3]**

**Larvae → Spawn → Brood → Solids Removal → Biofilter → Sump → Clean Lift → Header → Gravity back**

---

# **BILL OF MATERIALS (BOM)**

---

## **1\. CONTAINER & STRUCTURE**

### **Container**

* **(1)** 53' Reefer Container (used OK, sealed, functional insulation)  
* **(1)** Reefer control panel (kept for temp control, even if compressor used minimally)

### **Floor & Framing**

* **(12–16)** HDPE or marine plywood load-spreading plates (under tanks)  
* **(1)** Continuous shallow slope (1–1.5" total) achieved via:  
  * composite shims or tapered sleepers  
* **(1)** Central gravity trench OR wall-mounted gravity pipe rack

---

## **2\. TANKS (ALL GRAVITY-DRAINED)**

### **Broodstock Conditioning (Zone 2\)**

* **(2)** Round tanks, **400–500 gal**  
  * Center bottom drain (3–4")  
  * Sidewall surface skim (2")  
  * Lids (light control)

### **Spawning (Zone 3\)**

* **(1–2)** Round tanks, **200–300 gal**  
  * Gentle circular flow  
  * Egg-safe drains (screened)

### **Egg Incubation**

* **(8–16)** Vertical egg jars OR tray incubators  
* **(1)** Gravity-fed manifold with needle valves

### **Larval Tanks (Zone 4 – quiet end)**

* **(2)** Rectangular or round tanks, **50–100 gal**  
  * Blacked out  
  * Weir-style overflow (NOT standpipe)

### **Nursery Tanks**

* **(1–2)** Round tanks, **200–300 gal**  
  * Grading access  
  * Bottom drain \+ overflow

---

## **3\. GRAVITY PLUMBING (CORE OF THE SYSTEM)**

### **Pipe & Fittings (Schedule 40 PVC or HDPE)**

**Main drains**

* 4" pipe: \~40–60 ft (brood \+ spawn solids line)  
* 3" pipe: \~30–40 ft  
* 2" pipe: \~40–60 ft

**Returns / Overflows**

* 1.5" pipe: \~60–80 ft  
* 1" pipe: \~40 ft

**Fittings**

* Long-sweep 90s (avoid hard elbows)  
* True-union ball valves (CRITICAL)  
* Bulkheads (one per tank outlet)  
* Emergency overflow standpipes (one per tank)

**Screens**

* Egg-safe mesh screens (200–500 micron)  
* Removable solids guards

---

## **4\. MECHANICAL FILTRATION (NO PUMPS)**

### **Solids Removal**

* **(1)** Radial Flow Settler (RFS), **150–300 gal**  
* **(1)** Static screen or microscreen (gravity-fed)  
* **(1)** Sludge purge line to drain or tote

This is where perch waste is removed *before* it dissolves.

---

## **5\. BIOLOGICAL FILTRATION**

### **Moving Bed Biofilter (MBBR)**

* **(1)** Tank, **300–500 gal**  
* **K1 or similar media:** \~40–60% fill  
* **(1)** Low-pressure air diffuser ring  
* **(1)** Media retention screen

Gravity-in → gravity-out → constant water level

---

## **6\. SUMP & CLEAN WATER LIFT (ONLY PLACE WITH PUMPS)**

### **Sump**

* **(1)** Clean-water sump tank, **300–500 gal**  
* Level sensors (optional but recommended)

### **Pumps (low head, redundant)**

* **(2)** Identical low-head circulation pumps (1 duty, 1 backup)  
  * Sized for slow turnover, not velocity  
* Flexible unions \+ vibration isolation pads

---

## **7\. OXYGEN & AERATION (REDUNDANT)**

### **Air System**

* **(1)** Main blower (continuous duty)  
* **(1)** Backup air pump (battery or generator-backed)  
* Air manifolds per zone  
* Fine diffusers (larvae), coarse diffusers (brood)

### **Oxygen (optional but smart)**

* **(1)** Low-head oxygen cone OR inline diffuser  
* **(1)** Small O₂ cylinder \+ regulator (emergency)

---

## **8\. TEMPERATURE & THERMAL MASS**

### **Heating**

* **(2)** Inline electric water heaters (staged)  
* **(1)** Mixing valve / bypass loop

### **Thermal Bladder Walls (inside container)**

* **Segmented flat water bladders**, framed  
* HDPE liner behind bladders  
* Drain pan \+ leak sensor (CRITICAL)

These are **thermal mass**, not structural walls.

---

## **9\. CONTROLS & SENSORS**

### **Sensors**

* Temperature (3 zones minimum)  
* Dissolved Oxygen (brood \+ larvae)  
* Water level (sump)  
* Leak detectors (floor \+ bladder wall)

### **Controls**

* Simple PLC or aquarium controller  
* Alarm outputs (text/email)

---

## **10\. LIGHTING & BIOSECURITY**

### **Lighting**

* Dimmable LED strips (warm spectrum)  
* Independent photoperiod zones

### **Biosecurity**

* Footbath tray  
* Dedicated nets per zone  
* UV sterilizer on return line (CRITICAL for hatchery)

---

## **11\. EMERGENCY & FAILURE MODES (DO NOT SKIP)**

* **Gravity emergency overflow** from every tank  
* **Air-only survival mode** (no pumps)  
* **Manual drain bypass** around filters  
* **Generator inlet** (even small portable)

---

# **WHAT THIS SYSTEM CAN REALISTICALLY DO**

* Maintain **multiple brood lines**  
* Stage **controlled spawns**  
* Hatch and raise **thousands to tens of thousands of fingerlings/year**  
* Operate **quietly, safely, and predictably**  
* Integrate later with **greenhouse thermal tanks via heat exchanger**

---

## **NEXT STEP (optional but powerful)**

If you want, I can:

1. Convert this BOM into a **line-item cost estimate**  
2. Create a **build sequence (Week 1 → Week 6\)**  
3. Size it precisely to a fingerling target (5k / 20k / 50k)  
4. Design the **greenhouse dome tanks** as heat batteries that plug into this

Just tell me which one you want next — this is a *very* solid foundation.
